title: Cleanup, Partial Initialization, and Error Paths · Memory Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted failure-atomic resource constructor, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in failure-atomic resource constructor and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent which subresources are initialized so cleanup is valid after every acquisition point.
Release acquired resources in dependency-safe reverse order with idempotent or guarded cleanup.
Use structured goto cleanup when it centralizes unwinding without skipping initialization or obscuring ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Partial Initialization State
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug native-systems team handles memory case 5724. Produce bounded C evidence to unwind partially acquired resources in reverse order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent which subresources are initialized so cleanup is valid after every acquisition point.

Inspect the Partial Initialization State trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Error Cause Preservation
steps.instruction:
For this debug demonstration of cmem-error-cause-preservation, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-error-cause-preservation-1f87fj */" for changed case 3339. It must unwind partially acquired resources in reverse order, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.

C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve the first causal error while still recording cleanup failures and resource state.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Error Cause Preservation, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: The last cleanup error is always the most useful return value.
